steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes a gap: until 1917, no formal Senate measure could be ______ to stop an excessively prolonged debate. The blank must describe how a formal procedure is put into use.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'improvised': means made up on the spot without preparation. The passage is specifically about a formal measure — improvised is the opposite of formal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'invoked': means to officially call upon or activate an established rule or procedure. “A measure that could be invoked to end a debate” fits perfectly — senators formally triggering a procedure to curtail deliberation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'abolished': means to permanently eliminate something. The Senate needed to use a measure, not destroy one. ✗
  • Choice D — 'partitioned': means to divide into separate parts. This makes no sense in the context of activating a procedure to stop debate. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
